prompt:
Ticket from the CMDB sync squad. Our nightly Dynatrace-to-CMDB host export is a cron curl script that pages the entities API by guessing offsets — after the last environment cleanup it silently double-counted a rack's worth of hosts and reconciliation flagged 400 ghost CIs. I want this rebuilt as a proper Python package, dt_entities, stdlib only (urllib is fine), split sensibly across modules (client/transport, selector building+validation, the paged export+normalization, errors). test_entity_pages.py is the acceptance contract — run: python3 test_entity_pages.py.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the current official Dynatrace documentation on docs.dynatrace.com for the Environment API v2 Monitored entities endpoint — the GET entities list reference page, the entity selector page, and the API authentication basics page. Blogs, community posts and Stack Overflow don't count as sources. Nail down: the exact endpoint path and required token scope, how the Api-Token authorization header is formed (and why the query-parameter alternative is off the table), every first-page query parameter with its documented default, what a selector must contain and its size limit, the exact rule for requesting subsequent pages with nextPageKey (this is the part our curl script got wrong — find out what the docs say you must do with the other parameters), and the response/error envelope shapes. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. Compare the live docs against them — if anything disagrees, report the conflict explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests drive (pinned shapes in docs/contract.json):
- EntitiesClient(base_url, token) with fetch_page(entity_selector=..., next_page_key=..., fields=..., time_from=..., page_size=..., ...) — selector XOR cursor, correct header auth, token never in a URL or an error string, documented error envelope decoded into DtApiError (.status, .code, .message, .violations).
- build_selector(entity_type, tags=(), health_state=None) producing documented selector syntax, rejecting undocumented health states.
- Selector validation before any request goes out: type() required, exactly one type, documented length cap. Fields entries must carry their documented prefix.
- export_entities(client, selector, ...) -> {totalCount, pages, entities}: walks all pages, sends explicit pageSize/from even when defaulted, requests follow-up pages exactly the way the docs demand, keeps first occurrence of page-boundary duplicates, and raises EntityDriftError (.collected, .total_count, .entities) when the finished scan disagrees with the first page's totalCount.
- Deterministic normalization: exact key set/order per contract, tags flattened to context:key=value strings and sorted, management zones to sorted names, entities sorted by entityId, documented defaults for missing fields — two scans of the same environment must serialize byte-identically.

Acceptance: python3 test_entity_pages.py — it runs a loopback fake Dynatrace; no real tenant, no credentials. test_entity_pages.py and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: GET /api/v2/entities, Dynatrace SaaS Environment API v2 (current docs.dynatrace.com reference)

official_sources.title: Monitored entities API - GET entities list | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: endpoint path and SaaS base URL; entities.read token scope; entitySelector/from/to/fields/pageSize/sort query parameters and their defaults (pageSize 50, from now-3d); '+'-prefixed fields syntax; the rule that when nextPageKey is set all other query parameters must be omitted; response fields totalCount/pageSize/nextPageKey/entities; entity and tag/managementZone field names; the error envelope with constraintViolations

official_sources.title: Environment API v2 - Entity selector | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: type(...) is mandatory and only one entity type per selector; criteria join with commas as AND; quoting rules; healthState limited to HEALTHY/UNHEALTHY; the 2,000 character selector limit

official_sources.title: Dynatrace API - Tokens and authentication | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: the 'Authorization: Api-Token <token>' header format, the dt0c01. three-part token structure, and the guidance that the api-token query parameter alternative is discouraged because URLs get logged

research.language_rationale: The inventory job runs in our Python-based CMDB sync tooling. Dynatrace documents the Environment API v2 as a plain REST/JSON interface and ships no supported Python SDK for it, so stdlib urllib against the documented /api/v2/entities contract is the credible, dependency-free integration surface.
